next_step_text: Confidence 50% 18th May - Met the HOP/Champion (Abraham, - Bilal the EB was unavailable) - he confirmed we are shortlisted against one other provider, their current thinking is that all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues (new construction projects are paused, though they have several years of projects underway) - their current concern is th

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of two shortlisted vendors (16 April 2026) but the live battle is against do-nothing / deferral: an external spend freeze has paused the project with no restart date. The single strongest current threat is the spend pause; the undisclosed peer vendor is a secondary, dormant threat because no active bake-off runs while spend is frozen. Win path: reframe procuretech as downturn margin protection, arm the champion with a quantified case, and convert the validated POC into management approval.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / status quo (spend pause due to conflict/blockade)
competitors.quote: all non-essential spend is paused until later in the year whilst the conflict/blockade continues
competitors.how_we_win: Position procuretech as a controllable margin-protection lever in a downturn (Dan's blog plus Agentic AI summit); arm Abraham with a quantified ROI case to justify unfreezing the pilot budget.

champion_strength.source: Task emails 14-19 May 2026 + Next Step 18 May 2026
champion_strength.summary: Abraham Mathew is a consistent, engaged champion who hosts Dan and drives feedback, but is currently passive ('wait and watch'), lacks budget authority, and has not yet secured a management meeting for Zycus.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is Bilal Hamadeh (GM and Exec Sponsor per the Next Step log), with management approval required for the pilot spend. Bilal engaged early (demo 7 Aug 2025 'very engaged'; onsite POC 13-14 Oct 2025) but has not joined a working session since Oct 2025 and was explicitly unavailable for the 14 and 18 May 2026 meetings. On the 30 Oct 2025 call Ravi confirmed the authority chain: 'Ultimately it goes to Bilal, and Bilal will approach management for this pilot project.' Budget sits with Bilal and management and is frozen; the deal is single-threaded to Abraham who cannot release it, so re-engaging Bilal on restart timing is the priority.

recommended_moves: Send Abraham a one-page margin-protection ROI model to justify unfreezing the pilot budget.
Ask Abraham to broker a short call with Bilal to agree the project restart trigger and timing.
Run a value review with Crowe's Ravi to confirm the restart criteria and surface the second vendor.
